Neil Diamond was Father Jerry Hanley’s favorite artist. 
Neil was performing in Reno, Nevada in the 90’s.

I got tickets and called in a few favors and also scored Backstage Passes. 

Post show about 30 of us were huddled together and were excited to meet Neil. 
Sadly, he just gave us all a semi cursory wave and piled into an awaiting limo and sped away. 

I could sense Father Jerry’s disappointment in that long drive home that night.

This does look pretty damn good and I am excited for its release. Ever since I was a child I wanted to create and be a performer. To act, sing, play music, dance, write etc in the world of artistic storytelling and walk the path not only of highly admired/influential figures but that of what some of my relatives have strived amongst as well. I'm very happy to say that last year was an official initation point to getting my feet wet in the industry. Doing background work as an extra on 2 shows for major streaming companies and having the privilege to work as an extra in this film, specifically right up in the pit of the crowd during the concert sequence with Eddie Vedder and apparently making the cut with a brief appearance in the trailers. It was a very fun filled day that I shall not forget. Since then I've been gradually making my way to participating from one project to the next in independent productions in both short film and full length features as actual characters in the narrative. I am very grateful for all the blessings of support, opportunities, and shared experiences that have led me to where I am now and as to where I am going. Granted this is the early stages of my career, however it is, none the less, a point of acceleration that hopefully leads to nowhere except far and wide to onward and upward. If you have a dream, a burning desire that fills your soul with great passion for which you sense deep in your core and see yourself embracing to the fullest heights please do not commit amongst yourself the act of disservice by settling for  less than what you feel you're capable of achieving or where your potential may take you. Give yourself the grace of exploring and engaging the activities and submitting the kind of work that profoundly brings you the meaningful pleasure of joy and gratitude to be existing amongst this life. Have fun with the gifts you've been granted and endure such with the treasure of sharing it with those who give you love and opportunity to contribute a verse amongst this cosmic symphony we all play a part in.
Good for you 👍. Congratulations 👏!
​@rustymohr6652Thank you kindly, I really appreciate it!
That's amazing!  It's so inspiring that you are living your long-held dream now.  Keep reaching for the stars ✨️
​@user-dianejcmlwork in progress but thank you I deeply appreciate the acknowledgement in your sentiment. Be safe and take good care out here!🙏🏼

I was so excited to see this new trailer. But, it gives away major plot point. Why?  You had my money with Kate, Hugh, and the first trailer. Now a true "gasp out loud" moment has been given away. Bizarre.

Two points.  First, this is a true story, previously covered in a documentary with the same title.  What happened to Claire Sardina is no secret, revealed for the first time in this film.  A few seconds on Google will tell you.  Nothing is actually being spoiled here.

Second, the first trailer told almost nothing about the film except that Hugh Jackman and Kate Hudson play a couple who form a Neil Diamond tribute act.  It seems to me unremarkable that the second trailer would want to show a bit of the actual story.  If nothing else, that serves the purpose of giving the audience a proper idea of what they are going to see.

@rareimer I appreciate your feedback. As a rule I do not "study" movies to the point of knowing everything that happens before I go to the theater. What is the point? Do I want to be entertained or so I want to validate my research? The former, please! All I need is a broad overview. The first trailer told me everything I needed to know.
